text: (The camera pans forcibly to the side of the lectern, with sound.)
**Julian** pushes the door open and walks in.
He wears his hair in a side-parted slick-back.
His shirt is tailored, maybe a little too tight.
He has orange filter clips on his glasses.
She places a stack of orange books on the lectern.
In this small town, she was everything I imagined a professional city woman to be.
A business suit, black-rimmed glasses, a silver bracelet, and a faint professional smile.
(Minna timely closes the door and leaves.)
(He turns and scrawls a huge JULIAN on the whiteboard with a marker.)
Your teacher for this summer course.
The best month of the year.
In Latin, this means 'belonging to July'.
When I was studying abroad, on a beach over there...
I told myself, Julian, you must be like the scorching sun of July!
Burn away all mediocrity and cowardice!
English is the easiest language in the world!
As long as you dare to shout it out, you can overcome it.
(But it's clear the teacher is not satisfied.)
